# OneClick

> An end-to-end encrypted one-time URL redirect service. The browser
> generates a 256-bit AES-GCM key, encrypts the destination URL,
> uploads only the ciphertext, and embeds the key in the URL fragment
> so the server never sees it.

OneClick is a small, auditable demonstration of how to build a
one-time-link service where the server is honest-but-curious.

## Design in one sentence

A 32-byte AES-GCM key + 12-byte IV are generated client-side, the
destination URL is encrypted with the Web Crypto API, only the
ciphertext + iv + ttl is POSTed to the server, and the key travels
in the URL fragment (`#k=…`) which browsers never send in HTTP.

## Documentation

- [README.md](README.md): Quickstart, API surface, trust model.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Threat model and intentional non-goals.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md): Module-by-module reference.
- [.repobility/access.yml](.repobility/access.yml): Endpoint-by-endpoint
  authorization matrix.
- [docs/adr/](docs/adr/): Architecture Decision Records.

## Source files

- `main.go`: HTTP routes, security headers middleware, template render.
- `store.go`: SQLite-backed store with atomic click-consume.
- `templates/`: Go `html/template` files for the compose, view, and
  gone pages.
- `static/crypto.js`: Web Crypto API wrapper — AES-GCM keygen,
  encrypt, decrypt, base64url helpers.
- `static/compose.js`: Compose flow.
- `static/view.js`: Viewer flow — peek, confirm, consume, decrypt,
  redirect.
- `tests/*_test.go`: Go test suite — store, handlers, capability-URL
  authorization invariants.

## How to run

    go run .
    # → http://127.0.0.1:8080
